---
title: "Day 1 - Block 6"
subtitle: "Memory"
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "TI Workshop | D1 B6"
    bibliography: ../../../resources/workshop-citations.bib
    embed-resources: true
---

## Memory is leverage and risk {.center}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Persist only what you can defend.
:::

## Objective

- Separate working and persistent memory
- Mitigate stale memory risks
- Apply memory-safe writing (E4)

## Working vs persistent memory

| Type | Scope | Risk |
|---|---|---|
| Working memory | current task/session | stale context in long threads |
| Persistent memory | cross-session/project | privacy leaks, irrelevant carryover |

## Privacy red flags

- Early paper drafts
- Raw NDA data
- Personal data (names/emails/addresses)

## Safe alternatives

- Institutional secure chat instances
- Local models (e.g., Ollama)
- Cluster-hosted open models

## Writing workflow

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  A[Draft claim] --> B[Attach source]
  B --> C[Check citation scope]
  C --> D[Add uncertainty line]
  D --> E[Add disclosure line]
```

## Transparency and authorship

- Disclose model, version, provider
- Document prompt and verification
- LLMs are not authors

## Documentation template

```markdown
## Model Information
- Model:
- Provider:
- Date:
- Temperature:

## Usage Purpose
- Drafting / coding / retrieval

## Verification
- Checks performed:
- Remaining uncertainty:
```

## Hands-on (E4) {background-color="black"}

- Draft one evidence-backed paragraph
- Add claim-evidence table + disclosure
- Submit: `/docs/live-exercises?exercise=E4`

## Registry references {.smaller}

- Memory + learning risk: R024
- Appropriate reliance: R041, R042
- Safety warning signs: R051, R052

## Debrief

- Which sentence was hardest to verify?
- What evidence would falsify your claim?
- Where should delegation stop?
